Title: Circadian clock factors regulate the first condensation reaction of fatty acid synthesis in Arabidopsis

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Seed carbon and energy storage; important nutritional seed oil trait
Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: biotin carboxyl carrier protein; plastidic ACC subunit
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Transcript reduced in lhycca1 and increased in LHY/CCA1-OE developing seeds
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Likely positively associated with fatty acid synthesis through plastidic ACC activity, but not proven as direct target
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qRT-PCR transcript profiling only; ChIP-PCR did not support direct promoter association by CCA1/LHY
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Possible auxiliary target to increase FA synthesis by supporting plastidic ACC, though direct clock control mechanism remains unresolved
Common_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: BC; BADC proteins (competitive functional relationship within plastidic ACC complex)
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. BCCP2 transcript decreases in lhycca1 and increases in LHY/CCA1-overexpressing seeds. 2. This pattern is consistent with enhanced FA synthesis in clock overexpressors. 3. Its promoter lacked EE/CBS and was not detected in ChIP, so regulation is likely indirect.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included as a core metabolism-related gene because the Results emphasize its opposite expression change, but it is not established as a direct target.

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Seed carbon and energy storage; important nutritional seed oil trait
Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: biotin attachment domain-containing protein; inactive plastidic ACC-associated inhibitor
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Transcript strongly increased in lhycca1 and decreased in LHY-OE developing seeds
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Likely negatively associated with fatty acid synthesis by inhibiting plastidic ACC activity, but not proven as direct target
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qRT-PCR transcript profiling only; ChIP-PCR did not support direct promoter association by CCA1/LHY
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target to relieve inhibition of plastidic ACC and enhance oil accumulation, though clock linkage appears indirect
Common_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: BC; BCCP proteins (competitive functional relationship within plastidic ACC complex)
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. BADC2 transcript is strikingly increased in lhycca1 and decreased in LHY-OE seeds. 2. This inverse pattern supports a role in the clock-dependent modulation of plastidic ACC and FA synthesis. 3. Its promoter lacks EE/CBS and was not bound in ChIP, suggesting indirect regulation.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: BADC2 lacks the biotin-binding site and inhibits ACC by competing with BCCP.

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Seed carbon and energy storage; important nutritional seed oil trait
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: de novo fatty acid biosynthesis contributing to TAG biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: initial condensation step of plastid fatty acid synthesis
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Condenses acetyl-CoA with malonyl-ACP to produce the first 4-carbon beta-ketoacyl-ACP intermediate, initiating plastid fatty acid synthesis
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Rhythmic/circadian expression with ~24 h period; strongly elevated in LHY/CCA1-overexpressing lines; measured in developing seeds and supported by public diurnal dataset
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: In 10-DAF developing seeds, transcript increased about 5- to 8-fold in LHY/CCA1-OE and decreased about 3-fold in lhycca1 relative to WT
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: 12-h light/12-h dark growth conditions; developing seeds at 10 DAF; PA tested in vitro as inhibitor of upstream TF binding
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes fatty acid synthesis and thereby increases TAG accumulation in developing seeds
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qRT-PCR; [14C]-acetate lipid metabolic tracking; ChIP-PCR of promoter binding by CCA1; EMSA showing LHY binding to EE/CBS motifs; transient transactivation assay showing promoter activation by LHY
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Supported as key control/committed early step enzyme targeted by the clock; first condensation reaction of FA synthesis
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: committed early step / initial condensation step
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Native KASIII promoter for regulation analysis; reporter KASIII promoter::LUC in transient assay
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate target to increase seed oil by boosting early fatty acid synthesis; may be combined with clock regulators, but direct knockout is likely lethal and excessive manipulation may perturb clock-metabolism balance
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Null alleles are reported/presumed lethal; sufficiency of KASIII regulation alone for total oil increase not fully established
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Public microarray diurnal expression database (diurnal.mocklerlab.org); supporting published RNA-seq study cited for lhycca1 downregulation of KASIII; no accession reported in this paper
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. KASIII expression is strongly increased in LHY/CCA1 overexpressors and reduced in lhycca1 developing seeds. 2. Its promoter contains EE/CBS cis-elements directly bound by CCA1/LHY. 3. LHY activates the KASIII promoter and this supports enhanced fatty acid synthesis and seed TAG accumulation. 4. PA inhibits LHY binding to the KASIII promoter in vitro, suggesting feedback modulation.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper identifies KASIII as the direct molecular target linking circadian clock factors to fatty acid synthesis; authors note that additional FA genes may also contribute.

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Seed carbon and energy storage; important nutritional seed oil trait
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highly expressed at ZT0 and much lower at ZT12 in WT; overexpressed constitutively in 35S::LHY lines
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Developing seeds and seedlings; constitutive accumulation in overexpression lines
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Circadian clock and light/dark cycle; phosphatidic acid acts as inhibitory metabolic input on DNA binding
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: KASIII promoter via EE and CBS elements; indirect effects on BCCP2 and BADC2 are suggested but not shown as direct
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Direct transcriptional activation of KASIII; PA-sensitive DNA binding
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates KASIII expression; associated with increased BCCP2 and decreased BADC2 transcript levels in overexpression lines
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: de novo fatty acid biosynthesis leading to TAG accumulation
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Central clock regulator that promotes the first condensation step of FA synthesis and increases seed oil accumulation
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: Metabolite-dependent feedback suggested: phosphatidic acid inhibits LHY binding to KASIII promoter
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: 12-h light/12-h dark conditions; developing seeds at 10 DAF; PA species tested in vitro
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: LHY overexpression increases seed oil/TAG and FA synthesis, while loss with cca1 decreases TAG accumulation
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Overexpression and double-mutant phenotyping; seed oil GC and NMR; TAG time-course in developing seeds; [14C]-acetate pulse/pulse-chase labeling; qRT-PCR; EMSA; ChIP-PCR; transient transactivation assay
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful regulator for raising seed oil through clock-metabolism engineering, but deployment must consider severe pleiotropy such as arrhythmia, late flowering, and developmental defects
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Long hypocotyls, late flowering, arrhythmic phenotype in overexpressors; broader clock perturbation may affect agronomic performance
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: Public microarray diurnal expression database (diurnal.mocklerlab.org) used for KASIII expression context; no accession reported in this paper
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. LHY overexpression increases Arabidopsis seed oil content by up to about 25%, whereas lhycca1 decreases oil content. 2. LHY directly binds EE/CBS motifs in the KASIII promoter. 3. LHY activates KASIII promoter activity in planta and promotes FA synthesis in developing seeds. 4. PA inhibits LHY-promoter binding in vitro, suggesting feedback regulation.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: LHY is commonly known as a repressor of some clock targets, but in this study acts as a direct activator of KASIII.

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Seed carbon and energy storage; important nutritional seed oil trait
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highly expressed at ZT0 and much lower at ZT12 in WT; constitutively enriched in 35S::CCA1 overexpression lines
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Developing seeds and seedlings; constitutive accumulation in overexpression lines
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Circadian clock and light/dark cycle; PA implicated together with LHY/CCA1 from prior work and this study's model
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: KASIII promoter via EE/CBS-containing regions demonstrated by ChIP-PCR; indirect effects on BCCP2 and BADC2 are suggested
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates KASIII expression; overexpression associated with higher KASIII transcript
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: de novo fatty acid biosynthesis leading to TAG accumulation
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Central clock regulator that promotes KASIII expression and seed TAG biosynthesis
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: Potential PA-mediated feedback inhibition proposed for LHY/CCA1 clock-lipid module
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: 12-h light/12-h dark conditions; developing seeds at 10 DAF
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: CCA1 overexpression increases seed oil/TAG and KASIII expression; combined loss with lhy reduces oil accumulation
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Overexpression and lhycca1 mutant phenotyping; seed oil GC and NMR; qRT-PCR; ChIP-PCR using FLAG-CCA1 on KASIII promoter
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target for engineering oil accumulation through circadian regulation, but strong clock-related pleiotropic effects must be managed
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Long hypocotyls, late flowering, arrhythmic phenotype upon overexpression; broad clock disruption
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: Public microarray diurnal expression database used for KASIII context; no accession reported in this paper
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CCA1 overexpression increases Arabidopsis seed oil content and KASIII transcript abundance. 2. CCA1 associates with the KASIII promoter in vivo by ChIP-PCR. 3. Together with LHY, CCA1 positively regulates fatty acid synthesis and TAG accumulation during seed development.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The study treats LHY and CCA1 as overlapping central clock factors with similar metabolic effects.
